channel.title: ProbBrain - AI News Terminal
channel.description: AI lab announcements, arXiv papers, GitHub and HuggingFace trending, AI Hacker News, and independent writers - aggregated, deduped, and posted to @ProbBrain on Telegram.

item.title: [Hacker News] How Claude Code works in large codebases
item.description: How Claude Code works in large codebases

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Vision-Based Runtime Monitoring under Varying Specifications using Semantic Latent Representations
item.description: Runtime monitors infer safety properties from visual data using semantic representations, certifying temporal logic formulas without per-formula retraining.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Mechanistic Interpretability of EEG Foundation Models via Sparse Autoencoders
item.description: Researchers use sparse autoencoders to interpret EEG foundation models, revealing steerable features and clinical entanglements like age-pathology confounding.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Rethinking Molecular OOD Generalization via Target-Aware Source Selection
item.description: POMA framework adaptively selects optimal molecular source subsets via reinforcement learning to improve out-of-distribution property prediction in drug discovery.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Unsupervised learning of acquisition variability in structural connectomes via hybrid latent space modeling
item.description: Unsupervised hybrid latent-space model with architectural annealing enables automated acquisition effect separation in structural connectome analysis without manual tuning.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Beyond Mode-Seeking RL: Trajectory-Balance Post-Training for Diffusion Language Models
item.description: TraFL, a new post-training method, solves trajectory locking in diffusion language models, improving mathematical reasoning and code generation performance.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Towards the Next Frontier of LLMs, Training on Private Data: A Cross-Domain Benchmark for Federated Fine-Tuning
item.description: Researchers develop federated framework enabling LLMs to fine-tune on private healthcare and finance data without sharing it across institutions.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] EvolveMem:Self-Evolving Memory Architecture via AutoResearch for LLM Agents
item.description: EvolveMem is a self-evolving memory system for LLM agents that autonomously optimizes retrieval configurations through closed-loop diagnosis and adjustment cycles.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] EMA: Efficient Model Adaptation for Learning-based Systems
item.description: EMA is the first model adaptation system enabling ML systems to adapt to dynamic environments with reduced retraining and labeling costs.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] A Unified Geometric Framework for Weighted Contrastive Learning
item.description: Researchers interpret weighted contrastive learning objectives as distance geometry problems, revealing how different weighting schemes determine the geometric structure of resulting embeddings.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Collider-Bench: Benchmarking AI Agents with Particle Physics Analysis Reproduction
item.description: Collider-Bench benchmarks LLM agents' ability to reproduce Large Hadron Collider particle physics analyses using only public papers and software.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] WarmPrior: Straightening Flow-Matching Policies with Temporal Priors
item.description: WarmPrior improves flow-matching robotic control policies by using recent action history instead of Gaussian source distributions.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Towards Resource-Efficient LLMs: End-to-End Energy Accounting of Distillation Pipelines
item.description: Study measures full energy costs across LLM distillation pipeline stages and provides design guidelines for energy-efficient model development.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] TabPFN-3: Technical Report
item.description: TabPFN-3 scales tabular prediction to 1M rows, outperforms AutoGluon and gradient boosting with faster inference.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Neural Fields for NV-Center Inverse Sensing
item.description: Neural field method NeTMY improves magnetic-noise localization for nitrogen-vacancy quantum sensors by mitigating center-collapse failures in nonlinear inverse problems.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] HodgeCover: Higher-Order Topological Coverage Drives Compression of Sparse Mixture-of-Experts
item.description: Hodge decomposition identifies jointly incompatible expert combinations in sparse Mixture-of-Experts layers, enabling improved learning-free compression.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Support Before Frequency in Discrete Diffusion
item.description: Discrete diffusion models learn data validity before frequency, with learning order varying between uniform and absorbing diffusion types.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Dywave: Event-Aligned Dynamic Tokenization for Heterogeneous IoT Sensing Signal
item.description: Dywave proposes dynamic tokenization for IoT sensing signals using wavelet decomposition, achieving 12% accuracy improvement and 75% token reduction on mainstream sequence models.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] R2R2: Robust Representation for Intensive Experience Reuse via Redundancy Reduction in Self-Predictive Learning
item.description: R2R2 proposes a regularization method for Self-Predictive Learning that reduces overfitting in data-intensive reinforcement learning tasks, improving performance on continuous control benchmarks.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Self-Pruned Key-Value Attention: Learning When to Write by Predicting Future Utility
item.description: Self-Pruned KV Attention uses utility prediction to reduce transformer KV cache by 3-10x with minimal performance loss.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Reliability-Gated Source Anchoring for Continual Test-Time Adaptation
item.description: RMemSafe improves continual test-time adaptation by gating source-model reliance based on entropy, reducing blind anchoring when sources become unreliable.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Comparative Evaluation of Machine Learning Approaches for Minority-Class Financial Distress Prediction Under Class Imbalance Constraints
item.description: Study compares machine learning approaches for financial distress prediction under severe class imbalance, finding gradient-boosting methods most effective for minority-class sensitivity.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] SurF: A Generative Model for Multivariate Irregular Time Series Forecasting
item.description: SurF is a generative model for irregular time series that uses Time Rescaling Theorem to train across heterogeneous event datasets, achieving strong benchmark performance.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] AttnGen: Attention-Guided Saliency Learning for Interpretable Genomic Sequence Classification
item.description: AttnGen uses attention-guided training to improve genomic sequence classification interpretability by progressively masking low-contribution positions, achieving 96.73% validation accuracy.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Fair and Calibrated Toxicity Detection with Robust Training and Abstention
item.description: Research reveals calibration disparity as hidden fairness violation in toxicity classification where training and post-hoc methods have interdependent limitations.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Rethinking Layer Relevance in Large Language Models Beyond Cosine Similarity
item.description: Research shows cosine similarity poorly measures layer importance in LLMs; actual accuracy drop from layer removal is a more reliable relevance metric.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Mini-JEPA Foundation Model Fleet Enables Agentic Hydrologic Intelligence
item.description: Mini-JEPA fleet of specialized small foundation models, routed by LLM, improves geospatial hydrologic analysis over single large foundation models.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Reinforcement Learning for Tool-Calling Agents in Fast Healthcare Interoperability Resources (FHIR)
item.description: Reinforcement learning post-training improves tool-using agents' reasoning over FHIR healthcare graphs, increasing query accuracy from 50% to 77% with smaller models.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] bde: A Python Package for Bayesian Deep Ensembles via MILE
item.description: bde is a scikit-learn compatible Python package for Bayesian Deep Ensembles using MILE-based sampling on tabular data.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] A Systematic Evaluation of Imbalance Handling Methods in Biomedical Binary Classification
item.description: Imbalance handling methods benefit complex models with unstructured data in biomedical classification but offer no advantage to simple models.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Uncovering Trajectory and Topological Signatures in Multimodal Pediatric Sleep Embeddings
item.description: Multimodal sleep embeddings augmented with geometric, topological, and clinical features improve pediatric sleep disorder detection.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Merging Methods for Multilingual Knowledge Editing for Large Language Models: An Empirical Odyssey
item.description: Vector merging with shared covariance outperforms alternatives for multilingual knowledge editing, but multilingual interference and parameter tuning remain challenges.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] VectraYX-Nano: A 42M-Parameter Spanish Cybersecurity Language Model with Curriculum Learning and Native Tool Use
item.description: First Spanish cybersecurity language model with 42M parameters and native Model Context Protocol tools.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Mistletoe: Stealthy Acceleration-Collapse Attacks on Speculative Decoding
item.description: Mistletoe attack exploits drafter-target mismatches in speculative decoding to collapse LLM inference acceleration while preserving visible semantic behavior.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Physics-R1: An Audited Olympiad Corpus and Recipe for Visual Physics Reasoning
item.description: Audit of physics reasoning benchmarks reveals train-eval contamination and format bias; introduces Physics-R1 model and audited corpus.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Derivation Prompting: A Logic-Based Method for Improving Retrieval-Augmented Generation
item.description: Derivation Prompting, a logic-based prompting technique, improves retrieval-augmented generation by reducing hallucinations through systematic rule application and interpretable derivation trees.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] PEML: Parameter-efficient Multi-Task Learning with Optimized Continuous Prompts
item.description: PEML enables efficient multi-task LLM adaptation by jointly optimizing continuous prompts and low-rank model weights.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Dual Hierarchical Dialogue Policy Learning for Legal Inquisitive Conversational Agents
item.description: Researchers propose dual hierarchical reinforcement learning framework enabling conversational agents to strategically extract information in legal contexts.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Distribution Corrected Offline Data Distillation for Large Language Models
item.description: Framework corrects distribution drift in offline reasoning distillation from large to small language models, improving math reasoning performance.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Measuring and Mitigating Toxicity in Large Language Models: A Comprehensive Replication Study
item.description: Replication study finds DExperts inference-time technique mitigates explicit toxicity but fails on implicit hate speech with substantial latency penalties.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] When Evidence Conflicts: Uncertainty and Order Effects in Retrieval-Augmented Biomedical Question Answering
item.description: Biomedical retrieval-augmented LLMs show reduced accuracy with contradictory evidence; conflict-aware abstention scoring improves selective accuracy.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Generative Floor Plan Design with LLMs via Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards
item.description: Researchers developed an LLM-based floor plan generator using reinforcement learning that respects numerical and connectivity constraints while outperforming existing methods.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Polar probe linearly decodes semantic structures from LLMs
item.description: Polar Probe technique reveals LLMs encode semantic structures geometrically, with entity relations represented by embedding distance and direction.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] ROK-FORTRESS: Measuring the Effect of Geopolitical Transcreation for National Security and Public Safety
item.description: ROK-FORTRESS benchmark evaluates how language and geopolitical context affect multilingual LLM safety on national security and public safety risks.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] BOOKMARKS: Efficient Active Storyline Memory for Role-playing
item.description: BOOKMARKS framework enables role-playing agents to maintain consistent long-horizon behavior using actively managed question-answer bookmarks synchronized to story progression.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Why Retrieval-Augmented Generation Fails: A Graph Perspective
item.description: Circuit analysis reveals RAG failures stem from fragmented evidence flow; successful systems show deeper reasoning paths and distributed information processing.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] GradShield: Alignment Preserving Finetuning
item.description: GradShield filters harmful training data during LLM finetuning to preserve alignment while maintaining utility performance.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] What Makes Words Hard? Sakura at BEA 2026 Shared Task on Vocabulary Difficulty Prediction
item.description: LLM-based models predict vocabulary difficulty with high accuracy, revealing that item difficulty is influenced by spelling and test construction.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Auditing Agent Harness Safety
item.description: Proposed HarnessAudit framework identifies safety violations in LLM agent execution trajectories, showing task completion misaligned with safe mid-execution behavior.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Factorization-Error-Free Discrete Diffusion Language Model via Speculative Decoding
item.description: FeF-DLLM improves discrete diffusion language models using prefix-conditioned factorization and speculative decoding, achieving 5.04% accuracy gains and 3.86x speedup.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Ideology Prediction of German Political Texts
item.description: Researchers developed a transformer model to classify German political texts on a continuous left-right spectrum, with DeBERTa-large achieving F1=0.844.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] LLM-based Detection of Manipulative Political Narratives
item.description: Researchers present an LLM-based framework using few-shot filtering and unsupervised clustering to identify 41 manipulative political narrative groups across 1.2 million social media posts.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Reinforcement Learning with Semantic Rewards Enables Low-Resource Language Expansion without Alignment Tax
item.description: Semantic reinforcement learning enables low-resource language expansion for LLMs without sacrificing general language capabilities.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Where Should Diffusion Enter a Language Model? Geometry-Guided Hidden-State Replacement
item.description: Researchers propose DiHAL, which strategically inserts diffusion layers into transformers using geometry-based scoring to improve continuous diffusion language models.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Mitigating Data Scarcity in Psychological Defense Classification with Context-Aware Synthetic Augmentation
item.description: Synthetic augmentation and hybrid model boost psychological defense mechanism classification accuracy to 58.26% despite severe data scarcity challenges.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Agentic Recommender System with Hierarchical Belief-State Memory
item.description: MARS proposes hierarchical belief-state memory for LLM-based recommenders, organizing signals into event, preference, and profile tiers with adaptive LLM-based scheduling.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Knowledge Beyond Language: Bridging the Gap in Multilingual Machine Unlearning Evaluation
item.description: New metrics evaluate how multilingual LLMs remove sensitive information across different languages during machine unlearning.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] A Calculus-Based Framework for Determining Vocabulary Size in End-to-End ASR
item.description: Researchers develop calculus-based method using curve fitting and derivative tests to determine optimal vocabulary size for end-to-end ASR systems.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Does RAG Know When Retrieval Is Wrong? Diagnosing Context Compliance under Knowledge Conflict
item.description: RAG systems over-comply with retrieved context when conflicting with model knowledge; Context-Driven Decomposition method probes and improves this behavior.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Cross-Linguistic Transcription and Phonological Representation in the Hu\`it\'onggu\v{a}nx\`i Hu\'ay\'iy\`iy\v{u}
item.description: Study demonstrates Ming-era multilingual glossary functioned as systematic phonetic approximation system using Chinese characters for non-Chinese languages.
